metadata.title: youin | Spatial backlog for web apps
metadata.description: Click any part of your web app to create a project-scoped mark with screenshot, selector, browser context, and discussion attached.

notFound.asideNote: * Request matched no live route or document. Assets and deep links drift when URLs change between releases — return to dashboard or homepage to continue.
notFound.title: This route has no landing yet.
notFound.body: The URL might be mistyped, the page may have moved, or the link could be stale. Pick up from a known workspace surface and keep reviewing where your team actually ships work.
notFound.footer: youin — feedback on the live web

authShell.headline: Create the account. Start marking the product.
authShell.lead: YouIn keeps UI work attached to the live interface, then turns that context into tasks your team can actually resolve.
authShell.bullet1: Built for agencies and product teams working on live pages.
authShell.bullet2: Pins, selectors, screenshots, and discussion stay together.
authShell.bullet3: Invite reviewers after the workspace opens.

routeError.title: We couldn't finish loading this page.
routeError.body: The error has been logged. Try again — most failures here are transient (network blips, a slow query). If it keeps happening, head back to the dashboard and reopen the page.

bootstrap.title: Failed to load workspace
bootstrap.bodyFallback: Please try again or reload the page.

actions.triageSub: Unassigned open marks grouped by page

hero.title: Your product backlog, pinned to your actual UI.
hero.subtitle: YouIn turns your web app into a spatial backlog. Click any element, create the mark, discuss it in context, and keep screenshots, selectors, and browser metadata attached.

productStage.ariaLabel: Demo of YouIn triage dashboard with spatial marks on a live pricing page

rows.title: Pricing card CTA has low contrast
Empty state copy needs a better next step

productStage.detailTitle: Pricing card CTA has low contrast
productStage.detailBody: Captured selector, viewport, screenshot, and browser metadata.
productStage.mobileRowTitle: Pricing card CTA has low contrast

sync.title: Pricing card CTA has low contrast

problem.title: The work is spatial. The tools are flat.
problem.p1: UI work gets flattened into screenshots, Slack archaeology, and tickets that say which button? YouIn keeps the task attached to the exact interface moment that created it.
problem.oldLoopBody: Screenshot, annotate, paste in chat, explain the page, answer follow-up questions, repeat after the next deploy.
problem.withBody: Click the UI, create the task, keep the context alive where the work actually happens.

loop.title: Project-scoped work, mapped onto the product.

steps.title: Turn any element into a task
steps.body: Click a button, card, modal, table row, or empty state. YouIn captures the URL, selector, viewport, browser, and screenshot automatically.

steps.title: Keep the anchor alive
steps.body: Tasks stay tied to the interface with selector, viewport, and screenshot fallbacks, so deployment drift does not turn feedback into guesswork.

steps.title: Sync with your dev workflow
steps.body: Open a focused mark detail with the task, visual context, workflow status, and discussion thread without translating from a screenshot.

integrations.title: Use the extension, or bring YouIn directly into your app.
integrations.subtitle: The Chrome extension stays the fastest way to mark any page. For teams that do not want browser extensions, YouIn is being shaped around a hosted script and an npm package with the same capture model.

options.name: Chrome extension
options.body: Capture on any web app or staging site without changing the codebase.

options.name: Hosted script
options.body: Drop a tiny script into a first-party app so reviewers can create marks from inside your own product surface.

options.name: NPM package
options.body: Install a React-friendly package when teams want typed APIs, identity, route context, and deeper UI control.

integrations.contractBody: Allowed domains, current user identity, route and entity metadata, screenshots, selectors, and workspace routing should stay consistent across every install path.

who.title: Built for teams with expensive feedback loops.

personas.body: Turn client comments into precise UI tasks without asking anyone to write a ticket or describe a screenshot.
Let PMs, designers, and developers manage UI debt directly on the live product with statuses and views that fit the team.
Use the npm dev dependency as a spatial to-do list for your own app, with every fix living where the bug lives.

install.title: Stop describing the thing you can point at.
install.subtitle: The beta is free while the core workflow is being shaped with early teams. The next time someone spots UI work, turn that exact element into the task.
